senses.definition: in a way that is not diaphanous; without transparency or translucence; in a manner that is not clearly seen through or easy to perceive
examples: The fabric hung nondiaphanously, offering little hint of the wearer underneath.
She moved nondiaphanously, and the truth remained hidden.

senses.definition: the quality of not being diaphanous; lack of translucence or openness
examples: The nondiaphanousness of the curtain blocked the dawn light.
He valued nondiaphanousness in the arrangement, keeping ideas concealed.

senses.definition: not relating to diastasis; not divided by a gap (medical/technical use)
examples: The imaging showed nondiastasic tissue patterns.
In this context, the term indicates no diastasis is present.

senses.definition: not relating to diastasis; not showing separation or gap in a structure
examples: The study noted nondiastatic features of the specimen.
Researchers labeled the condition as nondiastatic.

senses.definition: not diathermanous; not showing a trait or property metaphorically understood as a shield or covering (rare/obscure coinage)
examples: The term appears in historical texts as a descriptor, though rarely used today.

senses.definition: not capable of being converted to or containing diazote (a hypothetical compound) or not subject to diazotization
examples: This chemical is nondiazotizable under the given conditions.

senses.definition: not dichogamic; in botany, not having the perfect timing of male and female parts for self-pollination
examples: The plant is nondichogamic, so its flowers can self-pollinate at any time.
Breeders noted the nondichogamic trait in the variety.

senses.definition: not dichogamous; not having the staggered maturation of male and female organs in flowers
examples: Nondichogamous plants reduce the likelihood of cross-pollination timing issues.
The species is selected for nondichogamous blooms.

senses.definition: the condition of not being dichogamous; flowers mature at similar times
examples: Nondichogamy can influence pollination dynamics in this species.
Researchers studied the effects of nondichogamy on seed set.

senses.definition: not dichotomous; not divided into two mutually exclusive parts
examples: The theory presents a nondichotomous view of the issue.
A nondichotomous approach recognizes multiple overlapping categories.

senses.definition: in a nondichotomous manner; without dividing into two opposed parts
examples: The model partitions data nondichotomously, allowing for overlap.
They argued nondichotomously about the issue.

senses.definition: the absence of dictation; not dictated or recorded by someone else
examples: The teacher noted a period of nondictation in the class.
Nondictation allowed students to express ideas freely.

senses.definition: not dictatorial; not ruling with an iron hand or autocratic authority
examples: The leader favored a nondictatorial approach to governance.
A nondictatorial management style improved team morale.

senses.definition: in a nondictatorial manner; without autocratic or tyrannical methods
examples: The council acted nondictatorially, inviting debate from all members.
She led nondictatorially, allowing consensus to form.

senses.definition: the quality of being nondictatorial; lack of autocratic authority
examples: The nondictatorialness of the organization encouraged participation.
Critics praised the nondictatorialness of the leadership style.

senses.definition: a dictionary that is not a dictionary in the usual sense (figurative); or a dictionary that does not exist
examples: The term nondictionary is rarely used and may appear in playful contexts.
Some students joke about building a nondictionary for fun.

senses.definition: not didactic; not intended to teach; not designed to instruct
examples: The lecture was engaging, nondidactic, and allowed spontaneous discussion.
The film presented ideas in a nondidactic way.

senses.definition: in a nondidactic way; not intended to teach
examples: The discussion proceeded nondidactically, with participants sharing stories rather than lectures.
She spoke nondidactically, inviting questions rather than delivering instruction.

senses.definition: not dietetic; not related to dietetics or nutrition guidance
examples: The cookbook includes nondietetic recipes suitable for celebrations.
Nondietetic advice was provided in the general wellness section.

senses.definition: in a nondietetic manner; not focused on dietetics
examples: The restaurant offered nondietetically prepared options for celebrations.
He spoke nondietetically about food, emphasizing enjoyment over restrictions.

senses.definition: the practice or activity of not following a diet; rejecting dieting
examples: Many people advocate nondieting as a healthy approach to eating.
She writes about nondieting and body positivity.

senses.definition: the act of not differentiating; lack of distinction between things
examples: The essay criticizes nondifferentiation between issues that require nuance.
Nondifferentiation in taxonomy can lead to confusion.

senses.definition: not differentiable; lacking a derivative at some point in calculus
examples: The function is nondifferentiable at x = 0.
This curve is nondifferentiable where it has a cusp.

senses.definition: the act or process of not differentiating; failure to distinguish
examples: Nondifferentiation can occur when categories are too broad.
The paper discusses the implications of nondifferentiation in data labeling.

senses.definition: not difficult; easy to accomplish or understand
examples: The task was nondifficult, taking only a few minutes.
For beginners, the steps are nondifficult to follow.
